title: PERF: double-parse in parse_incoming hot path
description:
parse_incoming() (transport.rs:606) deserializes every incoming text frame twice: first as serde_json::Value to inspect for 'id'/'method' fields, then as JsonRpcNotification or JsonRpcResponse via from_value(). This allocates an intermediate Value on every message.

A single-pass fix: deserialize into a thin envelope struct with optional 'id', 'method', and 'params'/'result'/'error' fields as raw serde_json::Value, then branch on field presence. Alternatively, use serde_json::from_str with a #[serde(untagged)] enum.

In practice: at human typing speed this is never measurable. At stress-test throughput (hundreds of messages/sec per client) the intermediate Value allocation would show up in a profiler. Filing for completeness; fix only if profiling confirms it matters.
close_reason: Pre-existing two-pass parse in parse_incoming predates the proxy feature; perf is negligible at human chat speed. Deferred as separate task if ever needed.
